frontier.hypothesis: F6 SOTN duplicated-statement-into-arms on non-arg5 co-live carrier (p73/p77/p79 per s69 greg extract).
frontier.mechanism: duplicated-statement-into-arms lifts reg_n_refs on p73/p77/p79 above p78's demoted priority=138 to offset the s60/s69 REG_EQUIV 4-cycle rotation; byte-neutral via cross-jump re-merge; SOTN-sanctioned 2026-07-01 carve-out permits this only under strict prereqs (byte-neutrality verified + exhaustion + FAKE annotation + layer-1/2 review).
frontier.next_probe: Draft 2-3 candidate duplications of a real statement referencing p73(tbl_125c) or p77(idx_1494-addr) into BOTH do_timeout arrival arms (success:->do_timeout: fallthrough and direct-goto-do_timeout: paths). Measure via sandbox --disable all; if byte-neutral AND lifts pri(carrier) above 138 in ALLOCDBG qty_compare, invoke layer-2 cheat-reviewer with FAKE annotation.

frontier.hypothesis: H2-extension ALLOCDBG conflict-list diff on non-arg5 co-live carriers under a byte-neutral C perturbation that ISOLATES a single p72/p73/p78/p79 refs/livelen delta.
frontier.mechanism: s69 named the rotation as atomic under qty_compare; s74/s75/s76/s77 confirmed simple structural/permuter-random reorderings don't move any single carrier's (refs,livelen). A C construct that adds an EXTRA use of ONLY tbl_125c or idx_1494 at a scheduled position could raise its refs by 1 without disturbing the other three carriers if the added use is at a site the RA already dominates.
frontier.next_probe: Sweep 2-3 candidate single-carrier extra-use insertions (a dead-in-outputs (void)*idx_1494 before or after the poll block; a redundant tbl_125c = tbl_125c; pointer self-assign near the check block) vs baseline ALLOCDBG dump; require byte-neutrality + isolated single-carrier refs delta before promoting to a sandbox measurement; FORENSICS modality, must be scheduled by the driver into an allocdbg session.

frontier.hypothesis: F12 Ghidra Pcode+SSA rederive remains operator-dependent.
frontier.mechanism: Ghidra's fold-vs-stage heuristics differ materially from m2c's RTL walker for variadic-call blocks with tbl-indexed dispatch; the m2c fixed-point saturation (13 sub-angles KILLED) does not preclude a distinct Ghidra reconstruction.
frontier.next_probe: OPERATOR ASK required: 'Do you have a Ghidra install with PS1/MIPS-R3000 support?' If not, F12 is a permanent operator-dependent frontier.
